title: A philosophy of home
description:
The household is a community, as much as the state, and ancient philosophy had much more to say about it than we think

- by Sandrine Bergès
hook: Home isn't shelter—it's a philosophical problem about belonging, and clay tells the story better than words.

title: “I Hope They Boo”: Tim Heidecker and Eric Wareheim Take Cannes
description: Laurel and Hardy, Martin and Lewis, Nichols and May… Tim and Eric. A double-act for the ages, Tim Heidecker and Eric Wareheim first teamed up as student
hook: Tim and Eric brought their beautiful violence to Cannes, and the festival had no idea what hit it.

title: Pamper Yourself This Pride with 1991’s ‘Vegas in Space,’ the Fiercest Drag Film on the Planet Clitoris
description: The late Doris Fish's legendary sci-fi film is a landmark of queer indie cinema for a reason.
hook: A 1991 drag sci-fi film so fearless it rewrote what queer cinema could be—and why you've never heard of it.

title: The World Cup and the Changing Psyche of the Haitian Diaspora
description: For Les Grenadiers and their fans, the game will be about the confrontation of a certain psychological displacement.
hook: Haiti's World Cup team isn't just playing soccer; they're embodying the psychological weight of diaspora itself.

title: Intimate Noises: Kim Gordon Conjures the Morning After at Amant, Brooklyn
description:
A new exhibition of the Sonic Youth co-founder’s visual art reveals a new hunger for connection – even as the work is marked by a wariness towards the aftereffects of desire

Kim Gordon, Jeanetta and Alex, 2026. Single-channel video, 09:12min. Courtesy the artist and Amant, Brooklyn, NY.

Kim Gordon is a famously gu
hook: Kim Gordon's visual art reveals what Sonic Youth never could: her hunger for connection disguised as distance.

title: A Graphic Novelist Captures the Lives of Vermont Lesbians Ahead of Their Time
description: Leaning on a rich written record, the graphic novelist Tillie Walden used nearby resources to visualize the true story of seamstresses who shared a home for decades.
hook: A graphic novelist resurrects Vermont lesbians from history: seamstresses who built a life together decades before it was safe.

title: The David Hockney Painting That Makes You Look, and Then Look Again
description: The tireless artist, who died this week, understood how to slow down time.
hook: Hockney's paintings don't just show you something—they teach you how to actually see, one brushstroke at a time.
